name: corruption_perception
description: Captures respondents' subjective beliefs about the prevalence, trajectory, and location of corruption in Mexico, reflecting a generalized cognitive appraisal of systemic corruption as a social phenomenon.

name: civic_tolerance_of_deviance
description: Measures the degree to which individuals normalize, justify, or accept minor rule-breaking and corrupt behaviors in everyday life, reflecting internalized ethical thresholds for what constitutes acceptable conduct.

name: prosocial_reporting_behavior
description: Assesses respondents' actual and hypothetical willingness to report or denounce corrupt, dishonest, or rule-violating behavior by others, capturing the behavioral dimension of anti-corruption engagement.

name: institutional_trust_and_efficacy
description: Measures trust in and perceived effectiveness of institutions—government, media, and enforcement bodies—in combating corruption and upholding legality, reflecting confidence in formal social control mechanisms.

name: anticorruption_civic_engagement
description: Captures respondents' exposure to, awareness of, and participation in anticorruption efforts including media campaigns, neighborhood meetings, and personal agency in resisting bribery demands, reflecting active civic engagement against corruption.
